what_it_does: This file is a channel attachment record that links a design-source channel entry to an image asset located at image://design-source/deck1-2026/p-16, storing only metadata (id, type, target, and added timestamp).
observations: The file contains only YAML frontmatter and no body content.
It declares an attachment_type of images.
The target uses a custom URI scheme image://design-source/deck1-2026/p-16.
The added timestamp is ISO 8601 with timezone offset.
The id uses an att- prefix and the channel is design-source.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter for structured metadata.
Id prefix att- indicates an attachment record.
Target asset referenced by a custom URI scheme.
concerns: No body or descriptive context is provided.
The image:// target cannot be verified from this file alone.
No author, source, or rationale for the attachment is recorded.
No versioning or removal metadata is present.
notes: A bare provenance stub that ties the design-source channel to a specific deck image at a single point in time.
questions: Who added this attachment and why?
Is the target asset still current or has it been superseded?
What does deck1-2026/p-16 correspond to in human-readable terms?
intention_signals: It appears to create a durable, traceable association between a design-source channel record and a specific image asset for later retrieval or audit.
novelty: Uses a custom image:// URI scheme to reference an asset within a deck/page path.
contribution: Provides a minimal but precise provenance record consisting of an attachment id, channel, target URI, and timestamp that a fused system could use to locate or audit the linked image.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML-only channel attachment record linking the design-source channel to the image asset at image://design-source/deck1-2026/p-16 with an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: To record when and where an image asset was attached to the design-source channel.
